title: Rootless podman fails to run Fedora 34 systemd aarch64 container in Travis CI
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug

**Description**

Rootless `podman run` fails when attempting to run `/usr/sbin/init` in Fedora 34-based ARM container in Travis CI.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Have Dockerfile.fedora-34 which says
```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:34
RUN dnf install -y --setopt=install_weak_deps=False /usr/sbin/init
```
2. In .travis.yml with
```
arch: arm64-graviton2
```
install podman 3.1.2-1 with
```
. /etc/os-release && echo "deb https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/ /" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/devel:kubic:libcontainers:stable.list && curl -L https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_${VERSION_ID}/Release.key | sudo apt-key add - && sudo apt-get update -y && sudo apt-get install -y podman containernetworking-plugins fuse-overlayfs uidmap slirp4netns
```
build image with
```
- podman build -t systemd -f Dockerfile.fedora-34 .
```
and attempt to run the container with
```
- podman run --name systemd -ti --rm systemd /usr/sbin/init
```

**Describe the results you received:**

https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/501607368
```
systemd v248-2.fc34 running in system mode. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX -APPARMOR +IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS +FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN +IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 +PWQUALITY +P11KIT +QRENCODE +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD +XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization podman.
Detected architecture arm64.
Welcome to Fedora 34 (Container Image)!
Failed to create /user.slice/user-2000.slice/user@2000.service/user.slice/podman-6674.scope/init.scope control group: Operation not permitted
Failed to allocate manager object: Operation not permitted
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object.
Exiting PID 1...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No error, container running fine.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

On AMD64, the same steps lead to working container in Travis CI:
https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/501611054

**Output of `podman version`:**

I did not run it but it's podman 3.1.2-1 installed from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04.

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

I did not run it but it's podman 3.1.2-1 installed from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04.

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

I did not run it but it's podman 3.1.2-1 installed from https://download.opensuse.org/repositories/devel:/kubic:/libcontainers:/stable/xUbuntu_20.04.

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is Travis CI Ubuntu 20.04 ARM64 VM.

comments.body: This worked fine with Fedora 33-based container in the same Travis CI setup.

Any chance you can get a `--log-level debug` added to the very end of that `podman run` command (after `/usr/sbin/init` - we want it to be an argument to systemd)? Systemd is very unhelpful about what actually went wrong at default log level.

https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/501711567
```
systemd v248-2.fc34 running in system mode. (+PAM +AUDIT +SELINUX -APPARMOR +IMA +SMACK +SECCOMP +GCRYPT +GNUTLS +OPENSSL +ACL +BLKID +CURL +ELFUTILS +FIDO2 +IDN2 -IDN +IPTC +KMOD +LIBCRYPTSETUP +LIBFDISK +PCRE2 +PWQUALITY +P11KIT +QRENCODE +BZIP2 +LZ4 +XZ +ZLIB +ZSTD +XKBCOMMON +UTMP +SYSVINIT default-hierarchy=unified)
Detected virtualization podman.
Detected architecture arm64.
Detected initialized system, this is not the first boot.
Welcome to Fedora 34 (Container Image)!
Hostname was already set to <91ead6682e99>.
Failed to add address 127.0.0.1 to loopback interface: Operation not permitted
Failed to add address ::1 to loopback interface: Operation not permitted
Failed to bring loopback interface up: Operation not permitted
Failed to read AF_UNIX datagram queue length, ignoring: No such file or directory
Setting 'fs/file-max' to '9223372036854775807'.
Failed to bump fs.file-max, ignoring: Permission denied
Setting 'fs/nr_open' to '2147483640'.
Failed to bump fs.nr_open, ignoring: Permission denied
Failed to read memory.limit_in_bytes cgroup attribute, ignoring cgroup memory limit: No such file or directory
Found cgroup on /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd, legacy hierarchy
Using cgroup controller name=systemd. File system hierarchy is at /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/user.slice/user-2000.slice/user@2000.service/user.slice/podman-6737.scope.
Failed to create /user.slice/user-2000.slice/user@2000.service/user.slice/podman-6737.scope/init.scope control group: Operation not permitted
Failed to allocate manager object: Operation not permitted
[!!!!!!] Failed to allocate manager object.
Exiting PID 1...
```
Alright, it's an EPERM trying to create a cgroup, specifically in Travis ARM64 (AMD64 works). Is this a travis VM, or container?
I do wonder about the underlying OS being different - possibly a different kernel or systemd config?

> Is this a travis VM, or container

It is a VM, defined with
```
virt: vm
arch: arm64-graviton2
group: edge
```
@giuseppe PTAL.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@giuseppe any ideas on this one?

looks like the user doesn't own the cgroup and then systemd refuses to run without cgroups.

Could you wrap the call to podman with `systemd-run --scope --user` and see if it makes any difference?

I also suggest to add `--log-level debug` to podman, so we can check if it is failing to create a new cgroup

Here's the run with
```
systemd-run --scope --user podman run --log-level debug --name systemd -ti --rm systemd /usr/sbin/init --log-level debug
```
https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/jobs/518998380

is the job triggered if I open a PR against your github repo?

Maybe it will be easier for me to debug what is going on
No. I'm pretty lot on the Travis CI credits at this point so I only run things manually when necessary.

I can't spot anything strange in the debug log.

Would be helpful to also print `find /sys/fs/cgroup/ -type d -exec stat \{\} \; && cat /proc/self/cgroup && ps auxww`
Here you go: https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/builds/230846266

the cgroup seems to be configured correctly, so I don't think it is an ownership problem.

I'd give it a try with seccomp and AppArmor disabled: `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined --security-opt apparmor=unconfined`

That worked: https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/builds/230860587

Now to figure out which of those did the trick.
So it turns out, both are actually needed to make systemd in rootless podman container work.

Could you look into /var/log/audit/audit.log to see what seccomp failures were being denied

ausearch -m seccomp

> So it turns out, both are actually needed to make systemd in rootless podman container work.

I take that back -- `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined` helped.
The seccomp failure should be fixed, we need to know which syscalls are being blocked.

When I try `ausearch`, I get
```
/home/travis/.travis/functions: line 109: ausearch: command not found
```
and I have no idea how to get it on that Travis CI Ubuntu. And when I try to grep it directly with `sudo grep -i seccomp /var/log/audit/audit.log`, I get
```
grep: /var/log/audit/audit.log: No such file or directory
```

I think I'll leave it with you guys, for the FreeIPA container `--security-opt seccomp=unconfined` is enough as a workaround.
Do you get journal logs?
I tried that with `sudo journalctl -l | grep -i seccomp` but did not see anything related to seccomp in the output: https://travis-ci.com/github/adelton/freeipa-container/builds/230870090
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Well unless you can get us the seccomp failure, we have no way to fix.  Closing, reopen if you descover what syscall is being blocked.
